name: Baked Barbecued Pork Buns (Cha Sui Bao)
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Cantonese barbecue pork, diced (about 1 lb.)
1 tbsp Cornstarch mixed with 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. Reheat in a 350 degree F. oven for 5 minutes, or microwave at high about 1 minute.
2. Prepare bread dough (recipe follows).
3. Mix together ginger, oyster sauce, hoisin, dark soy, sugar and water in a bowl.
4. Heat a wok over medium-high heat. Add oil. When hot, add onion; stir-fry until soft. Don't brown.
5. Add pork and stir-fry 30 seconds. Pour in sauce mixture, bring to a boil.
6. Stir cornstarch/water into a smooth mixture. Add to pork; cook, stirring until thick, about 15 seconds.
7. Add sesame oil. Remove to bowl; refrigerate until thoroughly chilled.
8. Cut dough in half. Form each half into a 12-inch long log; cut into 10 pieces. Roll each piece into a 4-inch circle.
9. Roll outer inch of each circle 1/8-inch thin; leave middle slightly thicker.
10. Place a dough circle in palm of your left hand. Put a big tablespoon of pork mixture in the middle.
11. With your right hand, bring up edge and make a pleat in it. Rotate circle a little and make a second pleat.
12. As you make each pleat, gently pull it up and around as if to enclose your thumb. Continue rotating, pleating and pinching.
13. Gently twist into a spiral. Pinch to seal. Place bun pleated side down on a parchment square.
14. Repeat with remaining dough and filling. Put buns 1 1/2 inches apart on a baking sheet.
15. Let rise until doubled in size, 30 minutes to 1 hour.
16. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Beat egg yolks with water and sugar; brush over buns.
17. Bake 20 minutes. Makes 20 buns.
Chinese Baked Sweet Bread Dough
- Put the yeast and 1 tablespoon of the sugar in a small bowl. Add 1/4 cup of the warm milk. Let stand 5 minutes, then stir to dissolve. If it does not foam and bubble, discard and use a fresh package of yeast.
- Stir in the egg, oil and remaining milk.
- Put the flour and remaining sugar in the work bowl of a food processor fitted with the metal blade. Process for 2 seconds.
- With the machine running, pour the warm milk mixture down the feed tube in a steady stream. Process until it forms a rough ball.
- If ball is sticky and wet, add a little more flour. Process a few seconds longer, or until dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
- Remove dough to a lightly floured board. Knead dough, dusting with flour to keep it from sticking, until smooth and elastic, about 2 minutes.
- Place in a large oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm spot until doubled, about 1 hour.
- Punch down dough and place on a lightly floured surface. It is now ready to form into rolls, buns or loaves. Makes enough for 20 barbecued pork buns.
